data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project structure. It's a minimal .NET 10 Web API with controllers. I need to:

1. Create a model for the request/response
2. Create a summarization service
3. Create a controller with the endpoint

Let me implement an extractive summarization approach - picking the most important sentences from the document text. This is a simple, self-contained approach that doesn't require external dependencies.

Let me create:
1. `Models/SummarizeRequest.cs` - request DTO
2. `Models/SummarizeResponse.cs` - response DTO
3. `Services/IDocumentSummarizationService.cs` - interface


4. `Services/DocumentSummarizationService.cs` - the extractive summarization logic that scores sentences and selects the most relevant ones
5. `Controllers/SummarizeController.cs` - the API endpoint that handles incoming requests

Then I'll register the service in Program.cs so it's available for dependency injection.

+using System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations;
+
+namespace DocSummary.Models;
+
+public class SummarizeRequest
+{
+    [Required]
+    [MinLength(1)]
+    public string DocumentText { get; set; } = string.Empty;
+
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Maximum number of sentences in the summary. Defaults to 3.
+    /// </summary>
+    [Range(1, 20)]
+    public int MaxSentences { get; set; } = 3;
+}
+

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-be3a4584e4e84449b7d9dd56803fe8b8/DocSummary/Services/DocumentSummarizationService.cs b/tmp/sv-be3a4584e4e84449b7d9dd56803fe8b8/DocSummary/Services/DocumentSummarizationService.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-be3a4584e4e84449b7d9dd56803fe8b8/DocSummary/Services/DocumentSummarizationService.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,70 @@
+using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
+
+namespace DocSummary.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Extractive summarization: scores sentences by word frequency and returns the top-ranked ones
+/// in their original document order.
+/// </summary>
+public partial class DocumentSummarizationService : IDocumentSummarizationService
+{
+    private static readonly HashSet<string> StopWords = new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
+    {
+        "a","an","the","and","or","but","is","are","was","were","be","been","being",
+        "have","has","had","do","does","did","will","would","shall","should","may",
+        "might","must","can","could","in","on","at","to","for","of","with","by",
+        "from","as","into","through","during","before","after","above","below",
+        "between","out","off","over","under","again","further","then","once",
+        "that","this","these","those","it","its","i","me","my","we","our","you",
+        "your","he","him","his","she","her","they","them","their","what","which",
+        "who","whom","how","not","no","nor","so","if","about","up","all","each",
+        "every","both","few","more","most","other","some","such","than","too",
+        "very","just","also","there","here"
+    };
+
+    [GeneratedRegex(@"(?<=[.!?])\s+", RegexOptions.Compiled)]
+    private static partial Regex SentenceSplitter();
+
+    [GeneratedRegex(@"\w+")]
+    private static partial Regex WordTokenizer();
+
+    public string Summarize(string documentText, int maxSentences)
+    {
+        var sentences = SentenceSplitter()
+            .Split(documentText.Trim())
+            .Where(s => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s))
+            .ToList();
+
+        if (sentences.Count <= maxSentences)
+            return string.Join(" ", sentences);
+
+        // Build word-frequency map (excluding stop words)
+        var wordFrequency = new Dictionary<string, int>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
+        foreach (var sentence in sentences)
+        {
+            foreach (Match match in WordTokenizer().Matches(sentence))
+            {
+                var word = match.Value;
+                if (!StopWords.Contains(word) && word.Length > 1)
+                    wordFrequency[word] = wordFrequency.GetValueOrDefault(word) + 1;
+            }
+        }
+
+        // Score each sentence
+        var scored = sentences
+            .Select((text, index) => new
+            {
+                Text = text,
+                Index = index,
+                Score = WordTokenizer().Matches(text)
+                    .Sum(m => wordFrequency.GetValueOrDefault(m.Value))
+            })
+            .OrderByDescending(s => s.Score)
+            .Take(maxSentences)
+            .OrderBy(s => s.Index) // preserve original order
+            .Select(s => s.Text);
+
+        return string.Join(" ", scored);
+    }
+}
+

+using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
+
+namespace DocSummary.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Extractive summarization: scores sentences by word frequency and returns the top-ranked ones
+/// in their original document order.
+/// </summary>
+public partial class DocumentSummarizationService : IDocumentSummarizationService
+{
+    private static readonly HashSet<string> StopWords = new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
+    {
+        "a","an","the","and","or","but","is","are","was","were","be","been","being",
+        "have","has","had","do","does","did","will","would","shall","should","may",
+        "might","must","can","could","in","on","at","to","for","of","with","by",
+        "from","as","into","through","during","before","after","above","below",
+        "between","out","off","over","under","again","further","then","once",
+        "that","this","these","those","it","its","i","me","my","we","our","you",
+        "your","he","him","his","she","her","they","them","their","what","which",
+        "who","whom","how","not","no","nor","so","if","about","up","all","each",
+        "every","both","few","more","most","other","some","such","than","too",
+        "very","just","also","there","here"
+    };
+
+    [GeneratedRegex(@"(?<=[.!?])\s+", RegexOptions.Compiled)]
+    private static partial Regex SentenceSplitter();
+
+    [GeneratedRegex(@"\w+")]
+    private static partial Regex WordTokenizer();
+
+    public string Summarize(string documentText, int maxSentences)
+    {
+        var sentences = SentenceSplitter()
+            .Split(documentText.Trim())
+            .Where(s => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s))
+            .ToList();
+
+        if (sentences.Count <= maxSentences)
+            return string.Join(" ", sentences);
+
+        // Build word-frequency map (excluding stop words)
+        var wordFrequency = new Dictionary<string, int>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
+        foreach (var sentence in sentences)
+        {
+            foreach (Match match in WordTokenizer().Matches(sentence))
+            {
+                var word = match.Value;
+                if (!StopWords.Contains(word) && word.Length > 1)
+                    wordFrequency[word] = wordFrequency.GetValueOrDefault(word) + 1;
+            }
+        }
+
+        // Score each sentence
+        var scored = sentences
+            .Select((text, index) => new
+            {
+                Text = text,
+                Index = index,
+                Score = WordTokenizer().Matches(text)
+                    .Sum(m => wordFrequency.GetValueOrDefault(m.Value))
+            })
+            .OrderByDescending(s => s.Score)
+            .Take(maxSentences)
+            .OrderBy(s => s.Index) // preserve original order
+            .Select(s => s.Text);
+
+        return string.Join(" ", scored);
+    }
+}

result.detailedContent: <command with id: server2 stopped>

data.content:
Everything works. Here's what was added:

**`POST /api/summarize`** — accepts JSON with `documentText` (required) and optional `maxSentences` (default 3, range 1–20).

**Files created:**
- `Models/SummarizeRequest.cs` / `SummarizeResponse.cs` — request/response DTOs with validation
- `Services/IDocumentSummarizationService.cs` — service interface
- `Services/DocumentSummarizationService.cs` — extractive summarizer that scores sentences by word frequency (excluding stop words) and returns the top-ranked sentences in original order
- `Controllers/SummarizeController.cs` — the API controller

**`Program.cs`** updated to register the summarization service via DI.
